architecture.summary: Dual-Process Agent (DPA) is a framework that enables continual learning in frozen LLMs through a two-stage memory-based mechanism. System 1 (fast) retrieves relevant context from explicit long-term memory and generates responses; System 2 (slow) reflects on interaction outcomes and writes curated updates back into memory. Memory is maintained as bulletized entries with utility statistics, and insertions are filtered by a conservative curator gate to prevent degradation. The model backbone remains frozen; all adaptation occurs in the external memory layer.

biblio.title: Towards Self-Evolving Agents: A Dual-Process Framework for Continual Context Refinement

claims.statement: DPA decomposes each interaction episode into two complementary processes: a fast System 1 that retrieves compact, relevant context from an explicit long-term memory and generates responses, and a slow System 2 that reflects on outcomes and writes curated updates back into memory.

DPA maintains bulletized memory entries with utility statistics and employs a conservative curator gate that filters generic, redundant, or conflicting insertions to prevent memory degradation over extended interactions.
DPA enables learning without modifying a frozen model backbone.

DPA consistently outperforms vanilla prompting and competitive baselines on both GPT-5.1 and Llama-3.1-8B backbones, achieving the best overall performance across multiple reasoning and knowledge-intensive tasks.
